blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ obstetric analgesia & anesthesia?
ยาที่ทีม OB-anesthesia ให้แม่ ทุกชนิด มีโอกาสข้าม placenta — และส่งผลต่อ neonate ที่เราต้องรับช่วงต่อใน 60 วินาทีแรกของชีวิต. Take-home pearls: (1) systemic opioids = neonatal respiratory depression, dose- + timing-dependent (meperidine fetal t½ 13–23 hr vs maternal 2–3 hr); (2) neuraxial analgesia (epidural/spinal/CSE/DPE) — most effective, ไม่เพิ่ม cesarean rate, ไม่ส่งผลต่อ Apgar/neurobehavior; (3) epidural-related maternal fever ~20% — ไม่ใช่ infection, ไม่ต้อง treat แต่ NEC complicates sepsis workup; (4) GA = LAST resort สำหรับ CS — aspiration risk ↑, failed intubation 1:533, mortality 1.7× neuraxial; (5) เวลา OB GA ≥ skin-to-delivery prolonged → expect lightly-anesthetized flaccid neonate → ventilate, ไม่ต้อง CPR, ฟื้นเร็ว
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
รู้ pain pathways ของ labor (T10–L1 first stage visceral; S2–S4 second stage somatic via pudendal)
เข้าใจ maternal physiologic changes ที่กระทบ anesthesia: CO ↑50%, MV ↑50%, FRC ↓20%, gastric pH ↓ → aspiration risk
เปรียบเทียบ systemic opioids: meperidine vs morphine vs fentanyl vs remifentanil สำหรับ labor analgesia
จำแนก neuraxial techniques: epidural / spinal / combined spinal-epidural (CSE) / dural puncture epidural (DPE)
รู้ evidence: Anim-Somuah Cochrane 2018, Wong NEJM 2005 (early epidural ไม่เพิ่ม CS), Chau Anesth Analg 2017 (DPE)
เข้าใจ epidural-related maternal fever: 20% prevalence, noninfectious cytokine-mediated, ไม่ส่งผล neonatal sepsis incidence
จัดการ neonatal effects of GA: induction agents (propofol/etomidate/ketamine), halogenated, NMBs, sugammadex
รู้ Table 14.1 — perioperative drugs และ breastfeeding: AVOID codeine/meperidine/tramadol, OK fentanyl/morphine
ตอบสนอง maternal cardiac arrest: 4-min rule → perimortem CS (Katz Obstet Gynecol 1986; AHA 2020)
เตรียม neonate ที่แม่ได้ GA: ทีมพร้อม, anticipate flaccid hypotonic newborn ที่ตอบสนองต่อ ventilation
1. Anatomy of Labor Pain

First stage (cervical dilation): visceral pain — afferents from lower uterine segment + cervix → hypogastric nerves → sympathetic chain → dorsal root ganglia T10–L1 (“dull, intense aching”, poorly localized)
Second stage (pushing/expulsion): somatic pain — afferents from vagina + perineum + pelvic floor → pudendal nerve → S2–S4 dorsal root ganglia (“sharp, well-localized”)
Implication: neuraxial blocks must extend from T10 → S2–S4 for full second-stage relief
2. Maternal Physiologic Changes — Anesthesia Implications

rows: CO ↑ 50% by 3rd trim (HR ↑15–25%, SV ↑25–30%); CO ↑ further 40% in 2nd stage; greatest CO ↑ immediately postpartum (75% — autotransfusion 300–500 mL/contraction). SVR ↓20%, BP ↓5–20%. Aortocaval compression universal by 13–16 wk → maintain LATERAL TILT 30°
Plasma vol ↑45–55% > RBC vol ↑20–30% → physiologic dilutional anemia. Tolerates blood loss 500 mL (vaginal) / 1000 mL (CS)
Mucosal edema/friability of pharynx-larynx-trachea → ventilation, laryngoscopy, intubation MORE DIFFICULT. Worse with preeclampsia + 2nd-stage pushing. Failed intubation rate 1:533 (D'Angelo 2014)
MV ↑45–50%, O2 consumption ↑20%, FRC ↓20% → RAPID DESATURATION during apnea. Pre-O2 essential before GA induction
Gravid uterus ↑ intragastric pressure; ↓ esophageal sphincter tone; placental gastrin ↓ pH; opioids/labor delay gastric emptying → ↑ ASPIRATION RISK with GA induction

↑ from 100 → 700 mL/min at term (10% of CO), 80% to placenta. Limited autoregulation. ↓ with hypotension, ↑ uterine venous P (supine, contractions, pushing). Phenylephrine PREFERRED over ephedrine (less fetal acidosis — Ngan Kee 2009)

blocks.text: 3. Placental Drug Transfer
Mostly passive diffusion for drugs < 1000 Da, nonionized, lipid-soluble
LIMITED transfer: succinylcholine, nondepolarizing NMBs, heparin, glycopyrrolate (highly ionized / large)
READILY cross: volatile anesthetics, opioids, benzodiazepines
Ion trapping: local anesthetics + meperidine cross nonionized → fetal lower pH → become ionized → accumulate in distressed acidotic fetus
4. Labor Analgesia — Options & Comparison
4.1 Nonpharmacologic
Modest benefit: acupuncture, acupressure, TENS, relaxation, massage
Not better than placebo: hypnosis, intradermal water injections
Continuous support (doula/family): Bohren Cochrane 2017 — ↓ pharmacologic analgesia use, ↓ length of labor, ↓ operative/CS, ↑ 5-min Apgar
4.2 Systemic Opioids — Comparison Table

rows: Worldwide most used; ↓ Apgar, ↓ SaO2, ↑ time to sustained respiration with shorter dose-to-delivery interval
Rare today; “morphine sleep” (10–15 mg IM + promethazine 25 mg) in latent labor — Maykin 2021 no impact on mode/outcomes
Fetal:maternal ratio 1:3; small IV doses 50–100 μg/hr — no Apgar/neurobehav diff vs no opioid
PCA option when neuraxial contraindicated. Liu meta 2014 — epidural superior. ↑ maternal desaturation

blocks.text: 4.3 Inhaled Nitrous Oxide (50:50 with O2)
Weak analgesic; intermittent self-administered; popular in UK/Canada/Australia/Scandinavia, growing in US
No effect on uterine contractility, mild maternal CV/respiratory depression
Neonatal: no adverse effect on Apgar, FHR, cord gases, neurobehavior (Rosen 2002, Likis 2014)
Rapid elimination — does NOT cause neonatal depression regardless of duration
5. Neuraxial (Regional) Analgesia
5.1 Local Anesthetics
Bupivacaine and ropivacaine most common for neuraxial labor analgesia
0.1% bupivacaine for labor; 0.5% bupivacaine, 2% lidocaine, 3% chloroprocaine for cesarean (denser block)
Co-administer opioid (e.g., fentanyl 2 μg/mL) for synergy and lower dose

LAST — Local Anesthetic Systemic Toxicity
Accidental large IV bolus → tinnitus, perioral tingling → seizure, arrhythmia, cardiovascular collapse, death. TREATMENT: ACLS + 20% IV lipid emulsion (1.5 mL/kg bolus → 0.25 mL/kg/min infusion). ASRA 2020 LAST checklist (Neal Reg Anesth Pain Med 2021).
5.2 Neuraxial Opioids
Epidural fentanyl/sufentanil: rapid analgesia, most opioid crosses to maternal plasma → potential respiratory depression dose-dependent
Intrathecal opioids: more potent but limited 2 hr; with bupivacaine 2.5 mg ↓ pruritus/nausea
HIGH-dose intrathecal sufentanil (7.5 μg): ↑ fetal bradycardia (OR 1.8 — Mardirosoff BJOG 2002), severe pruritus (RR 29.6) — uterine hyperactivity from rapid analgesia
5.3 Neuraxial Techniques — Comparison

rows: Continuous; titratable; can extend for CS
Easier, lower failure; useful precipitous labor / forceps assist
Fast onset + sacral coverage + catheter; higher satisfaction
Better sacral spread vs epidural; less pruritus/hypotension vs CSE; fewer top-ups
Newer technique; benefits over CSE in Chau 2017

blocks.text: 5.4 Effects on Labor & Mode of Delivery
Epidural prolongs 2nd stage by ~15 min (Anim-Somuah Cochrane 2018) — not harmful if FHR reassuring
Old data: ↑ instrumented vaginal delivery (RR 1.44); post-2005 trials NO significant ↑ (RR 1.19, 95% CI 0.97–1.46)
NO effect on cesarean delivery rate (Anim-Somuah Cochrane 2018)
Wong NEJM 2005: early epidural (< 4 cm) does NOT ↑ CS — ACOG/ASA: “maternal request is sufficient justification”
5.5 Maintenance Modalities
Continuous infusion vs PCEA vs PIEB (programmed intermittent epidural bolus)
PCEA + background infusion → best satisfaction, fewer clinician boluses, no ↑ adverse effects (Heesen Anesth Analg 2015)
PIEB → ↓ breakthrough pain, ↓ LA usage, ↑ satisfaction (Sng Cochrane 2018)
5.6 Contraindications & Complications
Absolute contraindications
Patient refusal
Infection at insertion site
Significant coagulopathy
Hypovolemic shock
↑ ICP from mass lesion
Inadequate provider expertise
HIV is NOT a contraindication (Hughes 1995)
Serious complications (D'Angelo Anesthesiology 2014, n > 250,000)
Less severe but common
Hypotension: most common; treat with lateral displacement, IV fluids, phenylephrine > ephedrine (less fetal acidosis — Kinsella consensus 2018)
PDPH (post-dural puncture headache): after accidental dural puncture; treat with analgesics or epidural blood patch
Inadequate analgesia requiring catheter replacement: 7% epidural, 3% CSE

Epidural-Related Maternal Fever — สำคัญต่อ neonatal sepsis evaluation
เกิดขึ้นใน ~20% ของ epidural labor analgesia (เดิมเชื่อว่าทุกคน ปัจจุบันรู้ว่าเฉพาะ subset). ไม่ใช่ infection — mediated by noninfectious inflammatory cytokines (Sharma Anesth Analg 2014 — ABx prophylaxis ไม่ป้องกัน). ไม่เพิ่ม WBC, ไม่เพิ่ม neonatal sepsis incidence (Arendt & Segal Clin Perinatol 2013). Implication for neonatologist: epidural-fever ใน chorioamnionitis evaluation — sepsis risk model (Kaiser EOS calc) ใช้ค่า peak temp ≥ 38.0°C เป็น risk factor — แต่ epidural fever alone ไม่เพิ่ม EOS risk ถ้าไม่มี clinical chorioamnionitis. ไม่ต้อง pump-and-discard, ไม่ต้อง treat แม่, แต่ต้องประเมิน infant clinically + risk factors เสมอ
5.7 Paracervical & Pudendal Blocks
Paracervical block: rare today, 15% fetal bradycardia  — no longer in ACOG 2019 PB 209
Pudendal block: for 2nd stage when neuraxial unavailable; high failure rate; spinal at ≥7 cm gives better relief (Pace 2004)
6. Anesthesia for Cesarean Delivery
6.1 Overview
Vast majority = neuraxial (less neonatal exposure, awake mom, neuraxial morphine for postop, avoid aspiration/airway risk)
GA reserved for: emergent (severe fetal bradycardia, uterine rupture, abruption), neuraxial contraindicated (coagulopathy, hemorrhage)
6.2 Three Anesthetic Approaches

rows: Indwelling labor catheter; convertible to surgical anesthesia
T4 to sacral block; failure rate 1.7–19.8% (higher in urgent cases — Bauer 2012). Epidural morphine for postop pain
Most common when no labor epidural
Easier, faster, more reliable. Hyperbaric bupivacaine + fentanyl + morphine. Greater hypotension risk → cohydration + phenylephrine
Pre-O2; rapid sequence induction; cuffed ETT. Fatality 1.7× neuraxial (Hawkins Obstet Gynecol 2011) — most from intubation failure

blocks.text: 6.3 Induction Agents
Propofol (2.5 mg/kg): UA:UV ratio 0.7; no neonatal effect at typical dose; high doses (9 mg/kg) → newborn depression. Hypotension if hemodynamically compromised
Etomidate (0.3 mg/kg): rapid onset, minimal CV effect (good for hemodynamic instability); can ↓ neonatal cortisol production < 6 hr (Crozier 1993)
Ketamine (1.5 mg/kg): sympathomimetic, ↑ BP/HR/CO; useful in compromise; no neonatal depression at typical dose; high doses ↑ uterine tone
6.4 Inhaled Halogenated Anesthetics
Sevoflurane / isoflurane / desflurane — readily cross placenta; potential ↓ uterine tone (atony) — typically partially replaced post-delivery
Cochrane Afolabi 2012: no difference in neonatal Apgar ≤ 6 or ≤ 4 at 5 min between neuraxial vs GA

FDA 2016 Warning — anesthetic neurotoxicity in developing brain
“Repeated or lengthy use of general anesthetic and sedation drugs during surgeries or procedures in children younger than 3 years or in pregnant women during the third trimester may affect the development of children’s brains.” Mechanism: GABA/NMDA receptor binding → neuronal apoptosis (animal data). HUMAN DATA INSUFFICIENT. De Tina Anesthesiol Clin 2017 — no link between urgent CS GA and later neurocognitive disability. Nitrous oxide NOT included in the warning. Single brief exposure for CS = low concern
6.5 Induction-to-Delivery vs Incision-to-Delivery

ข้อสำคัญสำหรับ neonatologist
Long induction-to-delivery interval = lightly anesthetized neonate (NOT asphyxiated). Long incision-to-delivery = uterine blood flow compromised → fetal asphyxia risk. Anesthetized neonate: flaccid, ↓ tone, cardiorespiratory depression — ventilate, expect rapid improvement; CPR usually unnecessary. หากไม่ดีขึ้น → search alternative cause
6.6 Neuromuscular Blocking Agents
Succinylcholine (1–1.5 mg/kg): highly ionized, poor lipid solubility — minimal placental transfer. Standard for RSI
Rocuronium (alternative): rapid onset 60–90 sec; reversed by sugammadex 16 mg/kg (α-cyclodextrin)
Sugammadex caveat: SOAP recommends avoidance in pregnant women undergoing nonobstetric surgery — may bind progesterone; safe at conclusion of CS (Richardson Anesth Analg 2020)
Neonatal NMB recognition: flaccid, no spontaneous ventilation, normal CV/color → ventilate up to 48 hr until drug excreted; reversal with neostigmine 0.06 mg/kg + atropine if needed
7. Breastfeeding After Anesthesia (Table 14.1)

rows:
“Sleep and Keep, ไม่ใช่ Pump and Dump” — Kraus J Womens Health 2020
ไม่แนะนำให้ pump-and-discard milk after anesthesia. ทันทีที่แม่ตื่น/รู้ตัว → ให้นมได้เลย. Resource: LactMed (Drugs and Lactation Database, NIH) — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK501922/

“Sleep and Keep, ไม่ใช่ Pump and Dump” — Kraus J Womens Health 2020
ไม่แนะนำให้ pump-and-discard milk after anesthesia. ทันทีที่แม่ตื่น/รู้ตัว → ให้นมได้เลย. Resource: LactMed (Drugs and Lactation Database, NIH) — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK501922/

“Sleep and Keep, ไม่ใช่ Pump and Dump” — Kraus J Womens Health 2020
ไม่แนะนำให้ pump-and-discard milk after anesthesia. ทันทีที่แม่ตื่น/รู้ตัว → ให้นมได้เลย. Resource: LactMed (Drugs and Lactation Database, NIH) — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK501922/

“Sleep and Keep, ไม่ใช่ Pump and Dump” — Kraus J Womens Health 2020
ไม่แนะนำให้ pump-and-discard milk after anesthesia. ทันทีที่แม่ตื่น/รู้ตัว → ให้นมได้เลย. Resource: LactMed (Drugs and Lactation Database, NIH) — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK501922/
Bupivacaine, etomidate, lidocaine, midazolam, N2O, propofol, ropivacaine, volatiles
Acetaminophen, fentanyl, hydromorphone*, morphine*, neuraxial opioids, NSAIDs (oral), oxycodone, remifentanil
Atropine, gadolinium, glycopyrrolate, iodinated contrast, NDMRs, neostigmine, succinylcholine

blocks.text: *Close monitoring recommended for hydromorphone & morphine — premature/apnea-prone infants need extra caution
AVOID rationale: codeine — CYP2D6 ultrarapid metabolizers → infant death (Kelly Pediatrics 2012); meperidine — neonatal cyanosis/apnea/bradycardia (Cobb 2015); tramadol — same CYP2D6 issue
8. Maternal Cardiac Arrest — Perimortem Cesarean

4-Minute Rule (AHA 2020 / Jeejeebhoy Circulation 2015)
Maternal cardiac arrest: ถ้า ROSC ไม่กลับมาภายใน 4 นาที → perimortem cesarean delivery (Katz Obstet Gynecol 1986) — เพิ่ม survival ทั้งแม่และลูก (decompresses aortocaval compression, restores venous return). Goal: delivery within 5 min of arrest. ทำที่ bedside, ไม่ต้องเข้า OR. Neonatologist + warm radiant warmer พร้อมที่ delivery point
9. Practical NICU Pearls — “Call จาก Labor Room”

ถามอะไรจาก anesthesiologist? (1) anesthetic type (epidural/spinal/CSE/GA), (2) GA induction agents + last dose timing, (3) opioids given (drug + cumulative dose + time before delivery), (4) magnesium (preeclampsia: ↑ neonatal hypotonia, hyporeflexia), (5) maternal hypotension episodes / pressor use, (6) maternal temp / suspected chorio

ถ้า maternal opioid PCA: expect potential respiratory depression — naloxone 0.1 mg/kg IV/IM (NRP 2021: discouraged routine use ; consider only ถ้า prolonged respiratory depression after adequate ventilation)

ถ้าแม่ได้ GA + prolonged incision-to-delivery: anticipate flaccid, hypotonic neonate → ventilate first, expect rapid improvement; if not — search other causes (hypovolemia, anemia from APH, CDH, NM disease)
Postdural puncture headache (PDPH) ในแม่: ไม่กระทบ neonate โดยตรง แต่อาจ ↓ feeding/bonding ในวันแรก — ส่งเสริม skin-to-skin + family-centered care
Neuraxial morphine ในแม่หลัง CS: ขนาดมาตรฐาน OK สำหรับ breastfeeding (LactMed); แต่ถ้า maternal opioid for postop pain เกินมาตรฐาน → monitor infant for sedation/poor feeding
10. 5 Bedside Pearls — สำหรับใช้พรุ่งนี้

rows:
PEARL 1 — Ion trapping
Local anesthetics + meperidine: nonionized in maternal blood → cross placenta → fetal acidosis → ↑ ionized fraction → trapped & accumulated in distressed fetus. หาก mom ได้ paracervical block หรือ meperidine + neonate มี acidosis → expect prolonged effect

PEARL 2 — Phenylephrine > ephedrine
Phenylephrine (α-agonist) LESS fetal acidosis than ephedrine (β-agonist) for treating maternal hypotension after spinal — ephedrine ↑ fetal metabolism + lactate (Ngan Kee Anesthesiology 2009; Kinsella consensus 2018)

PEARL 3 — Epidural fever ≠ chorioamnionitis
20% of laboring women on epidural develop fever — noninfectious cytokine-mediated. ABx prophylaxis ไม่ป้องกัน (Sharma 2014). Sepsis evaluation should be guided by clinical chorioamnionitis criteria (rupture, fetal tachycardia, uterine tenderness, foul lochia, leukocytosis) — not isolated maternal fever

PEARL 4 — “Flaccid baby after maternal GA” — usually NOT asphyxia
Long induction-to-delivery interval = lightly anesthetized neonate. Ventilate → rapid improvement. Long incision-to-delivery = real asphyxia risk. If no improvement with ventilation → think aspiration / maternal hypovolemia / CDH / congenital NM disease

PEARL 5 — Sleep & Keep, NOT Pump & Dump
Mom สามารถ breastfeed ได้ทันที ที่ตื่น/รู้ตัวหลัง anesthesia (general หรือ neuraxial). AVOID codeine, meperidine, tramadol. OK fentanyl, morphine (monitored), oxycodone, NSAIDs, acetaminophen. Resource: LactMed

blocks.text: 11. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Anim-Somuah Cochrane 2018 (CD000331): Epidural vs non-epidural — superior pain relief; +15 min 2nd stage; NO ↑ in CS rate; ↑ instrumented vaginal delivery in pre-2005 trials only (RR 1.44 → 1.19 post-2005)
Wong CA NEJM 2005;352:655 (n=750): Early (< 4 cm) neuraxial vs systemic opioid → NO difference in CS rate → ACOG/ASA recommend epidural at any time on maternal request
Chau A et al. Anesth Analg 2017;124:560 (RCT): DPE > epidural and > CSE — better quality, less pruritus/hypotension/hypertonus
Ngan Kee WD Anesthesiology 2009;111:506: Phenylephrine causes less fetal acidosis than ephedrine for spinal-induced hypotension
Kinsella SM Anaesthesia 2018;73:71: International consensus statement — phenylephrine first-line vasopressor for spinal CS hypotension
D'Angelo R Anesthesiology 2014;120:1505: Serious obstetric anesthesia complications — n>250,000 — high block 1:4,300, cardiac arrest 1:128,000
Hawkins JL Obstet Gynecol 2011;117:69: US anesthesia-related maternal mortality 1979–2002: GA fatality 1.7× neuraxial; intubation failure leading cause
Cochrane Afolabi BB 2012 (CD004350): Regional vs GA for CS — no neonatal Apgar/resuscitation differences
Bohren MA Cochrane 2017 (CD003766): Continuous labor support — ↓ analgesia use, ↓ CS, ↑ 5-min Apgar
Sharma SK Anesth Analg 2014;118:604: ABx prophylaxis does NOT prevent epidural-related fever — confirms noninfectious mechanism
Mardirosoff C BJOG 2002;109:274: Intrathecal opioid → ↑ fetal bradycardia OR 1.8 (uterine hyperactivity); pruritus RR 29.6
Andropoulos & Greene NEJM 2017;376:905: Anesthesia and developing brains — implications of FDA warning; insufficient human data
De Tina A Anesthesiol Clin 2017;35:69: GA in 3rd trimester — no link to childhood neurocognitive outcome (review)
Jeejeebhoy FM Circulation 2015;132:1747 / AHA 2020: Cardiac arrest in pregnancy — perimortem CS within 4–5 min; bedside, not OR
ACOG PB 209 (2019): Obstetric Analgesia and Anesthesia — comprehensive guideline; supersedes 2002 version
Practice Guidelines for Obstetric Anesthesia, ASA & SOAP 2016 (Anesthesiology 124:270): Maternal request alone is sufficient indication for neuraxial labor analgesia
Kraus MB J Womens Health 2020;29:1243: “Sleep and Keep” — debunks pump-and-dump myth; basis of Table 14.1
12. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: Pain pathways ของ first stage labor ผ่าน spinal cord ระดับใด? และ second stage?

ตอบ: First stage = T10–L1 (visceral, via sympathetic chain จาก uterine body + lower segment + cervix). Second stage = S2–S4 (somatic, via pudendal nerve จาก vagina + perineum). Neuraxial block ต้องครอบคลุม T10 → S2–S4 สำหรับ full second-stage relief
Q: ทำไม phenylephrine ถูกแนะนำมากกว่า ephedrine ในการ treat maternal hypotension หลัง spinal สำหรับ CS?

ตอบ: Ngan Kee Anesthesiology 2009: ephedrine (β-agonist) ↑ fetal metabolism + lactate → fetal acidosis; phenylephrine (α-agonist) คงค่า uterine perfusion โดยไม่ ↑ fetal metabolism. Kinsella consensus 2018 → phenylephrine first-line
Q: Meperidine: maternal half-life vs fetal/neonatal half-life? และทำไมต้องระวังเป็นพิเศษ?

ตอบ: Maternal t½ 2–3 hr vs fetal/neonatal t½ 13–23 hr (สูงกว่า 5–10 เท่า). Active metabolite normeperidine t½ 13–23 hr accumulates ในทารก หลัง repeated doses → ↓ Apgar, ↓ SaO2, prolonged time-to-respiration, ↓ neurobehavior, breastfeeding difficulty. Avoid in breastfeeding mothers (Cobb 2015 — neonatal cyanosis/apnea/bradycardia)
Q: Towner-style คำถาม: epidural analgesia เพิ่มอัตรา cesarean delivery หรือไม่? และเพิ่ม instrumented vaginal delivery หรือไม่?

ตอบ: Anim-Somuah Cochrane 2018 + Wong NEJM 2005: epidural ไม่เพิ่ม CS rate ไม่ว่าให้ที่ <4 cm หรือ later. Instrumented vaginal: เดิม RR 1.44 (pre-2005) → post-2005 RR 1.19 (95% CI 0.97–1.46) ไม่ significant เพราะใช้ dilute LA + opioid ลด motor block. ACOG/ASA: maternal request alone = sufficient indication
Q: Differential ของ “fever in laboring woman with epidural” — เป็น chorioamnionitis ไหม?

ตอบ: Epidural-related fever เกิดใน ~20% ของแม่ที่ใช้ epidural — noninfectious, cytokine-mediated (Arendt & Segal 2013; Sharma 2014 — ABx prophylaxis ไม่ป้องกัน). ไม่เพิ่ม WBC, ไม่เพิ่ม neonatal sepsis incidence. Implication: ใช้ clinical chorio criteria (fetal tachycardia, uterine tenderness, foul discharge, maternal leukocytosis, ROM > 18 hr) มาประเมิน — ไม่ใช่ fever isolated. Neonate evaluation per institutional sepsis risk model (Kaiser EOS calculator)
Q: DPE (dural puncture epidural) ต่างจาก CSE อย่างไร? ทำไม Chau 2017 แสดงว่า DPE ดีกว่า?

ตอบ: CSE: intrathecal medication ฉีดผ่าน spinal needle หลังจาก locate epidural space → spinal analgesia เริ่มทันที + epidural catheter สำหรับ maintenance. DPE: เจาะ dura ด้วย spinal needle (25g pencil-point) แต่ ไม่ฉีดยาเข้า intrathecal space — ใช้ dural hole เพิ่ม translocation ของ epidural medications. Chau Anesth Analg 2017 (RCT): DPE > epidural และ > CSE — better sacral spread, less asymmetric block, ↓ maternal pruritus, ↓ hypotension, ↓ uterine tachysystole
Q: 4-minute rule ในการช่วย maternal cardiac arrest คืออะไร?

ตอบ: ถ้า ROSC ไม่กลับมาภายใน 4 นาที → perimortem cesarean delivery (Katz 1986; AHA 2020). Goal: delivery ภายใน 5 นาทีจาก arrest. ทำที่ bedside, ไม่ย้าย OR. Reasons: (1) decompress aortocaval compression → restore venous return + maternal CO; (2) ↑ chance of ทั้งแม่และลูกรอด . Neonatology team + radiant warmer + full NRP equipment ต้องพร้อมที่ delivery point
Q: Cochrane Afolabi 2012 พบอะไรเมื่อเปรียบเทียบ regional vs GA สำหรับ CS ในด้าน neonatal outcome?

ตอบ: 16 studies — ไม่มี significant difference ใน Apgar ≤ 6 หรือ ≤ 4 ที่ 5 นาที, หรือ need for resuscitation. Conclusion: ไม่มีหลักฐานว่า neuraxial superior for neonatal outcome. แต่ neuraxial แนะนำเพราะ maternal benefits (less aspiration, awake mom, neuraxial morphine for postop)
Q: FDA 2016 warning เรื่อง general anesthesia ในเด็ก < 3 ปี และในแม่ตั้งครรภ์ trimester 3 — neonatologist ควรกังวลแค่ไหน?

ตอบ: Warning based on: GABA/NMDA receptor binding → neuronal apoptosis ใน animal studies. HUMAN DATA INSUFFICIENT. De Tina 2017 — no link between urgent CS GA และ later neurocognitive disability. Single brief exposure for CS = LOW concern. Concern เพิ่มขึ้นเมื่อ repeated หรือ lengthy exposures (>3 hr or multiple). N2O ไม่อยู่ใน warning. Bottom line: communicate with parents เปิดเผย แต่ไม่ delay emergent CS เพราะกลัว neurotoxicity
Q: Sugammadex คืออะไร และทำไม SOAP แนะนำหลีกเลี่ยงในหญิงตั้งครรภ์ที่ทำ nonobstetric surgery?

ตอบ: Sugammadex = α-cyclodextrin ที่ inactivates aminosteroid NMBs (rocuronium, vecuronium) — ขนาด 16 mg/kg IV → rapid reversal ภายใน 2–3 min. SOAP 2019: AVOID ในตั้งครรภ์ เพราะ binds free progesterone → theoretically alter pregnancy length. OK เมื่อใช้ end of CS (Richardson Anesth Analg 2020 — effective + safe). Insufficient evidence for early postpartum + breastfeeding
Q: Etomidate มี neonatal effect อะไร?

ตอบ: Etomidate (0.3 mg/kg) = decreased neonatal cortisol production < 6 hr (Crozier Br J Anaesth 1993) — adrenal suppression. Clinical significance ยังไม่แน่ชัด — ไม่ส่งผลต่อ neonatal outcome ในการศึกษาใหญ่. ใช้ใน hemodynamic instability เพราะ minimal CV effect (เปรียบเทียบกับ propofol → hypotension)
Q: ทารกแรกเกิดที่ “flaccid, no spontaneous ventilation, normal CV/color” หลังแม่ได้ GA + NMB — diagnosis และ management?

ตอบ: Diagnosis: neonatal neuromuscular blockade จากการที่ แม่ได้ NMB (large/repeated doses ของ rocuronium/vecuronium/cisatracurium ข้าม placenta แม้ว่าปกติจะข้ามน้อย). Differentiate from asphyxia: baby มี normal CV function + good color (asphyxia → bradycardia + cyanosis). Management: ventilatory support up to 48 hr; reversal with neostigmine 0.06 mg/kg + atropine; nerve stimulator confirms blockade
Q: Anesthesia drugs ใดที่ AVOID ใน breastfeeding และทำไม?

ตอบ: AVOID: (1) Codeine — CYP2D6 ultrarapid metabolizers สามารถ produce dangerous morphine levels → infant death (Kelly Pediatrics 2012); (2) Meperidine — prolonged neonatal half-life + active normeperidine → cyanosis/apnea/bradycardia (Cobb 2015); (3) Tramadol — same CYP2D6 issue; (4) Diazepam — long t½, accumulates. OK: fentanyl, morphine (monitored), oxycodone, hydromorphone (monitored), neuraxial opioids, NSAIDs (oral), acetaminophen, all anesthetics except diazepam
Q: Lactation safety resource ที่ neonatologist ควรรู้คืออะไร?

ตอบ: LactMed (Drugs and Lactation Database) — part of NIH National Library of Medicine Toxicology Data Network. URL: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK501922/ — search drug → infant exposure level, adverse effects, alternatives. Use เมื่อ counseling moms about postpartum medications + breastfeeding
Day 15 Preview

Avery Chapter 15 — Perinatal Transition and Resuscitation
พรุ่งนี้เข้า Part V — Care of the Neonate. Ch.15 = หัวใจของ delivery room: physiology of fetal-to-neonatal transition (lung fluid clearance, PVR drop, ductus closure), NRP 2020 algorithm (initial steps → PPV → CC → epi), oxygen titration (SpO2 targets), umbilical cord management (DCC vs UCM — Katheria/Tarnow-Mordi APTS NEJM 2017), therapeutic hypothermia (NICHD/CoolCap/TOBY trials), preterm resuscitation special considerations
